title: Light Venezuelan Mini Pudding
description: Tangy, pillowy, and diabetic-friendly, this individual-mini-cocotte dessert is a Venezuelan delight.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together sweetened condensed milk, lime juice, and egg yolks until well combined.
Add the heavy cream and vanilla extract to the bowl and whisk until smooth.
Place the mixture in the top of a double boiler set over simmering water and cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 170°F.
Remove the top of the double boiler from the heat and stir in the unsalted butter until melted.
Strain the pudding mixture into 4-6 individual-mini-cocotte dishes.
Place the dishes in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the dishes.
Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes, or until set.
Remove the dishes from the water bath and let cool to room temperature.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving.
